skill.name: email-sequence
skill.description: Create, audit, and optimize lifecycle email sequences for onboarding, nurture, retention, billing, win-back, and campaigns.
skill.summary: Plan email flows with clear triggers, timing, copy, calls to action, and metrics.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell, crypto, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and ordinary email marketing language, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Misread as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting for output examples, not Ruby or shell command execution. No command string, interpreter call, or user input execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are plain Markdown fences and template text. There is no Ruby code or shell execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain terms such as description, design, desperation, and destination. They do not reference DES, MD5, SHA-1, encryption, hashing, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text is email marketing guidance. No cryptographic API, algorithm name, or security function appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines discuss email timing, paid conversions, trials, and audience segments. They do not collect host data, enumerate users, inspect processes, or query operating system state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is business copy guidance. There are no commands, APIs, or instructions for discovering local system information.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines mention email value, helpful links, and referral sharing. They do not perform DNS lookups, port scans, HTTP requests, or network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are normal marketing recommendations. No network tooling, endpoints, sockets, or request code is present.

content.value_statement: Email programs often lose focus when each message has too many goals. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan clear lifecycle sequences with timing, copy, and metrics.
actual_capabilities: Plans welcome, nurture, onboarding, retention, billing, win-back, and campaign email sequences.
Defines triggers, goals, timing, exit conditions, and segment rules for each sequence.
Drafts subject lines, preview text, body copy, and clear calls to action.
Provides audit checklists for lifecycle email coverage across customer stages.
Suggests email testing priorities and core performance metrics.
limitations: Does not connect to email service providers or send messages.
Does not verify legal compliance for specific regions or industries.
Needs audience, offer, product, and performance context for best results.
Benchmarks are general guidance and may not match every market.

use_cases.title: Launch a Product Welcome Flow
use_cases.description: Create a multi-email onboarding sequence that helps new users reach their first useful result.

use_cases.title: Improve Lead Nurture Campaigns
use_cases.description: Turn a lead magnet or webinar follow-up into a structured sequence that educates and converts.

use_cases.title: Recover Inactive Customers
use_cases.description: Design re-engagement and win-back emails with timing, segmentation, and respectful messaging.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Welcome Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with core product and audience details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-email welcome sequence for [product]. The audience is [audience], the signup trigger is [trigger], and the primary goal is [goal]. Include timing, subject lines, preview text, body copy, and one CTA per email.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Lead Nurture Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for pre-sale education and conversion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a lead nurture sequence for people who downloaded [lead magnet]. The offer is [offer], the sales cycle is [length], and common objections are [objections]. Include sequence strategy, segmentation notes, email drafts, and metrics to track.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Existing Email Program
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using current lifecycle coverage and performance data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this email program: [current emails]. Identify missing lifecycle stages, weak CTAs, timing problems, and personalization gaps. Recommend priority fixes and propose new emails where coverage is missing.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Behavior-Based Lifecycle System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for triggered and segmented lifecycle email architecture.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a behavior-based lifecycle email system for [product]. Use these events: [events]. Define triggers, segments, suppression rules, exit conditions, copy angles, test ideas, and success metrics for each sequence.

output_examples.input: A trial welcome sequence for a project management app.
output_examples.output: A 7-email plan with immediate welcome, quick win, feature education, social proof, objection handling, advanced tips, and trial conversion messaging.

output_examples.input: A re-engagement sequence for inactive paid users.
output_examples.output: A 4-email flow with check-in, value reminder, helpful update, and final preference confirmation, plus segment rules and measurement guidance.

output_examples.input: An audit of lifecycle email coverage.
output_examples.output: A prioritized checklist covering onboarding, retention, billing, usage, win-back, and campaign gaps, with suggested next emails.

best_practices: Give each email one primary job and one main call to action.
Use behavior and lifecycle stage to make timing and content relevant.
Track activation, click, unsubscribe, conversion, and revenue metrics by sequence.
anti_patterns: Sending every contact the same generic sequence without segmentation.
Adding multiple competing offers or calls to action in one email.
Using urgency, discounts, or win-back messaging without a clear customer reason.

faq.question: Can this skill write full email copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft subject lines, preview text, body copy, and calls to action for each email.

faq.question: Does it send emails or connect to an ESP?
faq.answer: No. It creates strategy and copy only. You must implement the sequence in your email platform.

faq.question: What email types does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers welcome, nurture, onboarding, retention, billing, usage, win-back, and campaign emails.

faq.question: Can it audit existing sequences?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your current emails, timing, segments, and performance data for a focused audit.

faq.question: Does it handle compliance requirements?
faq.answer: It can remind you to use consent and unsubscribe practices, but it does not provide legal advice.

faq.question: What context improves output quality?
faq.answer: Share the audience, trigger, offer, conversion goal, brand tone, current emails, and performance benchmarks.
